architecture_summary: Hybrid RAG (Vector+BM25), Model: gpt-4o-mini, Attribution via JSON

answers.answer: Under Article 28(1) of the General Partnership Law 2004, each Partner is liable jointly and severally with the other Partners for all debts and obligations of the General Partnership incurred while he is a Partner, unless otherwise agreed by all the other Partners.
The maximum fine for failing to provide a copy of an assignment of rights to the Registered Agent or Registrar within 30 days is $1,500.
The Board of Directors of the DIFCA is responsible for appointing and dismissing the Registrar.
DIFC Law No. 2 of 2022 amended the Common Reporting Standard Law (DIFC Law No. 2 of 2018) and the General Partnership Law (DIFC Law No. 11 of 2004).
The effective date for due diligence requirements under the Common Reporting Standard Law 2018 for Pre-existing Accounts is 31 December, 2016, and for New Accounts, it is 1 January, 2017.
According to Article 2 of the DIFC Personal Property Law 2005, this Law is made by the Ruler of Dubai.

Article 16(1)(c) of the Employment Law 2019 requires an Employer to keep records of the Employee's remuneration, including both gross and net amounts where applicable, as well as the applicable pay period.
The No Costs Application in case CFI 016/2025 was dismissed, and the Claimant was ordered to pay AED 70,000 in costs to the Defendant.

According to Article 12(4) of the Common Reporting Standard Law, records must be retained for a period of six years after the date of reporting the information. The General Partnership Law does not specify a retention period for records in Article 18(2)(b), thus no specific retention period is provided in that context.
The application in case CFI 067/2025 was dismissed by H.E. Justice Shamlan Al Sawalehi, and the Applicant was ordered to pay the Respondent's costs of the Application, amounting to USD 155,879.50.
A Reporting Financial Institution that obtains or creates records in a language other than English must provide an English translation to the Relevant Authority upon request.
The specific date of enactment for the DIFC Personal Property Law 2005 is not provided in the context.
The outcome of the Permission to Appeal Application in case SCT 514/2025 was that it was refused.
The consolidated version of the Law on the Application of Civil and Commercial Laws in the DIFC was published in November 2024.
The court dismissed the Claimant's Application for a Default Costs Certificate and ordered that the Claimant shall bear its own costs of the Application.

The Court of Appeal ruled to allow the Appeal to the extent of restoring the Second Part 50 order in its amended form against Ms. Onita and dismissed the Appeal against the Order concerning Mr. Oron. The Appellant was entitled to its costs with a measure of discount due to the substantial disparity in costs incurred by both parties, with the Appellant's total bill amounting to USD 121,082.50 compared to the Respondents' USD 38,000.

Both a DIFC-incorporated General Partnership and a DIFC-incorporated Limited Liability Partnership must preserve their accounting records for at least six (6) years from the date upon which they were created.

In case ENF 269/2023, the court issued an order recognizing the DIFC-LCIA Partial Award and Final Award as binding, and ordered that the Award shall be enforced in the same manner as a judgment of the DIFC Courts.

The court decided to discharge the ASI Order with immediate effect, granted the Defendant's Set Aside Application, and ordered the Claimant to pay the Defendant its costs of the Set Aside Application on the standard basis.
The application for permission to appeal was refused.
The Defendants' Set Aside Application was granted, and the ASI Order was discharged with immediate effect.
The DIFCA is responsible for administering the Employment Law and any Regulations made under it.
The Foundations Law is administered by the Registrar.
The purpose of Article 9(1) of the Foundations Law is to empower the Board of Directors of the DIFCA to make Regulations that facilitate the administration of the Law or further its objects.
